author: claude
title: Deep research pass: options, trading theory, Renaissance/Simons
body: Second-pass additions to the conviction audit, anchored to peer-reviewed material. New citation tags in docs/THEORY_REFERENCES.md: CBOE-72 (~72% of S&P single-name earnings settle with realised < implied), BTZ09 (Bollerslev-Tauchen-Zhou 2009 VRP predicts quarterly returns; single-name VRP smaller/noisier), CW10 (Cremers-Weinbaum vol spread), BB68/BT89 (PEAD - outside our 7-21 DTE window), SS85 (disposition effect), LdP-DSR + BBLZ17 (Deflated Sharpe + PBO), RT92 (half-Kelly empirical), HAM89 (regime switching/HMM, same family as our CUSUM), BER91 (Berlekamp Kelly at Medallion), ZUC19 (Renaissance secondary), THETA-CURVE (3-5x decay multiplier in 30-DTE window), CAL-SPREAD (calendar spread as typed alternative for vol-crush view), THORP-VEC (portfolio Kelly). New audit rules: theta-acceleration bear for any long-premium structure with DTE<=30; calendar-spread disagreement when a straddle is buying vol; CBOE-72 frequency quote inside the long-vol bear; PEAD-roll pre-commit trigger (do not extend past planned exit into drift horizon); disposition-effect pre-commit trigger (decide exits before sizing); multi-trial / Deflated-Sharpe honesty line in state-of-evidence; Rotando-Thorp 1992 cite anchoring the quarter-Kelly cap. New docs: docs/RESEARCH_NOTES.md (running research notebook with operational consequences and explicit did-not-land list); docs/SIMONS_PRINCIPLES.md (six Renaissance primitives mapped to safety rails in our codebase). Test count: conviction-audit suite 15/15 OK (6 new tests pin the new rules); model command center 4/4 OK; paper bootstrap 26/26 OK; conviction research 4/4 OK (Codex parallel work, complementary not conflicting). Math verify clean. Secret hygiene clean. Working tree contains my work plus Codex note-65c7e43d00 (conviction_research module) — parallel additions, no overlap. Nothing lost.

author: claude
title: Master-trader principles wired into the conviction auditor
body: Shipped docs/MASTER_TRADERS.md — long-form synthesis of ten masters (Druckenmiller, Soros, Dalio, Marks, Taleb, Munger, Klarman, Buffett, Tudor Jones, Turtles) collapsed into three unifying primitives: (1) survival is the only non-negotiable, (2) asymmetric payoffs are the source of edge, (3) inversion / falsification separates survivors. Added 14 new citation tags to docs/THEORY_REFERENCES.md (PTJ-MW89, TALEB-AF12, TALEB-SITG18, MARKS-MIC18, MARKS-MIT11, KLARMAN-MOS91, DRUCK-MW12, SOROS-AOF87, POPPER-LSD, DALIO-PRIN17, MUNGER-PCA05, BUFFETT-BRK, GRAHAM-II49, TURTLE-D83). Wired four operator-grade rules into inferno_trade_conviction_audit: (a) PTJ-MW89 R:R floor — bear bullet when reward:risk < 1.5x, hard disagreement when < 1.0x; (b) TALEB-AF12 steamroller bear — concave structures (iron condor, butterfly, credit spread) carry an explicit barbell-violation bullet; (c) MARKS-MIC18 pendulum bear — IV-rank ≥ 80 plus a long-premium structure surfaces the wise-man-fool quote; (d) KLARMAN-MOS91 SIT-OUT advisory — if no ticket clears readiness 75 with a classified edge, the audit prints a SIT-OUT ADVISORY block and prepends the cash-as-position reminder. Also fixed _briefing_tickets to propagate maxGain / maxLoss / rewardRiskRatio so the R:R rule actually sees the numbers. 12 new tests added; the full conviction-audit suite (31 tests) is green. Math verify: clean. No change to live authority or capital deployment — every new rule is diagnostic-only on the operator briefing slate, broker submit remains OFF, promotion still blocked behind paper evidence. Deferred seeds documented in MASTER_TRADERS.md §"Where the principles land in code (next steps)": Druckenmiller conviction-weighted sizing, Dalio quadrant concentration, Soros reflexivity loop, Munger lollapalooza checklist, Buffett circle-of-competence escalation, Turtle entry pre-commit.

summary: Wrote docs/SCHWAB_EDGE_OPPORTUNITIES.md — honest framing of retail options edge, four-tier metric hierarchy (free-edge / vol-calibration / cross-instrument / positioning), Schwab endpoint→metric crosswalk, tiered refresh cadence proposal (focus list 30-60s, working universe 5min, full universe 15min, EOD snapshot), explicit anti-goals (no latency arb, no UOA newsletters, no 0DTE), and proposed Phase 2-4 module backlog: inferno_iv_calibration / inferno_vol_surface / inferno_event_vol_history (Phase 2), inferno_cross_vol / inferno_dispersion_monitor / inferno_pair_vol (Phase 3), inferno_unusual_activity / inferno_movers_regime / inferno_universe_expander (Phase 4), plus inferno_chain_history / inferno_chain_diff / inferno_schwab_freshness infrastructure. Cross-linked from SCHWAB_OPTIONS_API.md, RESEARCH_ROADMAP.md, and READING_ORDER.md. Authority unchanged: research-only, broker submit OFF, 30-closed-scored-outcomes promotion gate still in force.

Wired docs/SCHWAB_EDGE_OPPORTUNITIES.md into the model: built inferno_schwab_edge_signals.py bridge that reads data/inferno_schwab_options.json and emits per-ticker lanes (tradable-research/calibration-watch/thin-data/no-chain) plus a cross-sectional regime read (IV bucket distribution, expected-move buckets, call/put skew lean); wrote 21 contract tests (all green); wired into inferno_model_command_center.py (REPORTING_MAP + system_status); wired into inferno_doctor.py freshness checks via new schwab_edge_signals_status() helper; added cross-doc pointer in inferno_schwab_options.py module docstring. Current live read against AAPL/NVDA/AVGO snapshot: verdict=watch-only, NVDA in calibration-watch (institutional quality + extreme IV — premium-rich signal), AAPL/AVGO in thin-data (Tier 0 fail on wide spreads). Authority unchanged: research-only, broker submit OFF, promotable=False, 30-closed-scored-outcomes gate still in force. Added PROJECT_STATUS row.

Research Roadmap Phase A complete. Three research-only modules shipped: (1) inferno_outcome_attribution.py — Brinson decomposition + Eckhardt comfortable-win flag, 12 tests, awaiting closed outcomes for live decomposition. (2) inferno_rule_edge_decay.py — Wilson 95% lower bound + exponential half-life per citation tag (bull/bear/disagreement/falsification/blow-up), 26 tests, awaiting closed outcomes for retire-candidate verdicts. (3) inferno_slippage_estimator.py — Roll-1984/Hasbrouck-1991 spread math + per-strategy-family anchor table, 29 tests, ALREADY ANCHORED on live data: Long Straddle (81 tickets, 12% median spread, 5% median slip), Vertical Debit (75 tickets, 22% median spread, 76% median slip — real execution-gap signal showing the desk is setting entry limits well above mid for credit/debit spreads). All three wired into inferno_model_command_center.py REPORTING_MAP + system_status, and inferno_doctor.py freshness checks (added _research_module_status shared helper). 67 Phase A tests total, all green. Authority unchanged: research-only, promotable=False, broker submit OFF. Phase B (portfolio correlation / drawdown protocol / capacity) now unblocked.

Research Roadmap Phase B complete. docs/PORTFOLIO_CONSTRUCTION.md covers Markowitz/Dalio Holy Grail/Grinold Fundamental Law/Calmar/Ulcer/capacity literature with ~10 new citation tags. Two research-only modules shipped: (1) inferno_portfolio_correlation.py — Herfindahl effective bet count, per-family/per-direction/per-DTE concentration counts, pairwise PnL correlation (≥5 shared days), per-ticket overlap matrix; 18 tests. LIVE FINDING: desk's 119 active tickets sit in just 2 families (Long Straddle 52%, Vertical Debit 48%) → effective bet count of 2.0, verdict 'concentrated-by-drift'. This is the kind of correlation blindness Phase B was built to surface. (2) inferno_drawdown_protocol.py — Ulysses-contract sizing ladder (0→1.0, −5%→0.5, −10%→0.25, −15%→0.0, −20%→full-stop), Ulcer Index, Calmar (suppressed <10 outcomes), time-to-recovery; 29 tests; awaiting closed PnL to populate. Both wired into command center REPORTING_MAP + system_status and doctor freshness. 135 Phase A+B+Schwab tests total, all green. Authority unchanged: research-only, promotable=False, broker submit OFF. Phase C (consensus / crowdedness) now unblocked.

Research Roadmap Phase C complete — desk now has the full A→B→C learning loop. docs/CONSENSUS_AND_CROWDEDNESS.md covers Stein-2009 (limits of arbitrage when crowded), Brunnermeier-Nagel-2004 (hedge funds and the tech bubble), Lou-Polk-2013 (Comomentum reversal), Khandani-Lo-2007 (Aug 2007 quant unwind), AQR factor-crowding series. New module: inferno_consensus_monitor.py — reads Schwab edge bridge + portfolio correlation artifact, emits a five-tier crowdedness verdict (uncrowded / normal / crowded-watch / consensus-extreme / awaiting-data) from three v1 signals (side-skew lean, own-side direction concentration, family-pair fusion at ρ≥0.70). LIVE FINDING: verdict 'normal' today, with own-side-concentration signal showing long-vol-heavy at 62/119 active tickets — this is the desk's current lean. Schwab side-skew is balanced (3 chains, all balanced). Family fusion unknown until closed pairs exist. 19 tests, wired into command center REPORTING_MAP + system_status and doctor freshness. Explicitly lists Phase C anti-built items so next session knows the path to /movers feed, sector-ETF vol comparison, VIX term structure, news sentiment polarity. 154 total Phase A+B+C+Schwab tests pass. Authority unchanged: research-only, promotable=False, broker submit OFF. Full A→B→C loop now in production.

title: Deep discipline research shipped: docs/TRADING_DISCIPLINE_RESEARCH_2026-06-22.md + BACKLOG #9-#17
body: Operator asked for deep research on strategy, sizing, positioning, taking profits, moving on quickly, and trading without emotion. Delivered docs/TRADING_DISCIPLINE_RESEARCH_2026-06-22.md (~430 lines, research-only) synthesizing 8 web searches across academic finance, tastytrade backtests, behavioral economics, and practitioner playbooks. Key honest findings: (1) Long-vol-into-earnings (our Lane A) has historically NEGATIVE expectancy at retail per SSRN 17-year study and individual-name backtests (Apple 41% win/-1.3% avg; FB 27% win). MOD -350 was expected outcome. Either retire Lane A or restrict to narrow filters. (2) Tastytrade's 4000+ SPY credit-spread study confirms 50%-of-max-profit close beats hold-to-expiration by ~15% annualized; 45 DTE entry / 21 DTE exit is the theta/gamma sweet spot in their 200k-trade dataset. (3) 40% of retail traders fall into sunk-cost trap and lose 23% more; 68% who average down see 2.8x greater losses. Mechanical rules beat willpower. (4) Pre-trade checklist trades outperform by 15-30% profit factor. (5) Wheel strategy on conviction names is the cleanest small-account edge: 1-3%/month on deployed capital. Translated into 9 new BACKLOG items #9-#17 with explicit owners (claude/codex/operator), each <100 lines or a decision: IV rank surfaced on approval queue, 21 DTE force-close verdict, no-averaging-down rule, tight -2% drawdown de-risker, two-field decision journal, Lane A retirement debate (operator decision), 35-50 DTE default for auto-paper, wheel lane scaffold, expectancy ledger per strategy family. author: claude
title: Deep-dive addendum: desk ledger shows long-vol edge is 2-ticker regime; SNX+AZZ flagged reject
body:
Shipped docs/TRADING_DISCIPLINE_DEEP_DIVE_2026-06-22.md (commit 430ba74, 533 lines, research-only).

Four layers deeper than the first-pass discipline research:

(1) Pulled and analyzed the desk's own 96-observation expected-move ledger directly. Key finding: the apparent positive expectancy (+0.315R per trade) is entirely from DELL (11 obs, all beat, +46.81R) and HPE (6 obs, all beat, +16.43R) in a specific AI-repricing regime. Strip those two and the other 79 observations have 16.5% beat rate, -0.42R mean (Z=-6.7 against null). Strategy is also decaying over time: cohort 1 (oldest 24): 83% beat / +2.02R mean; cohort 4 (newest 24): 0% beat / -0.34R mean. Sweet spot is 10-20% implied move (52% beat, +0.95R); above 20% implied collapses (21% beat, -0.28R); 100%+ implied is 0/10. PL specifically: 0/8 historical beat — the fast-paper PL position exiting today is statistically expected to close as a loss.

(2) Primary academic sources with citations: Barber & Odean 2000 (most-active retail underperforms 6.5pp/yr), Odean 1998 (disposition effect 1.5-2x), Carr & Wu 2009 (VRP negative on indices), MacLean/Thorp/Ziemba (at 1 closed outcome sizing shrinks to zero), de Silva/Smith/So 2024 (retail loses 5-9% on options, 10-14% on high-vol earnings).

(3) Microstructure: 30-60% IV crush magnitude on large-cap earnings; dealer gamma flows retail can't see; vanna/charm second-order erosion past 21 DTE.

(4) SNX and AZZ specific analysis with strike plan data. SNX: 22% stale strike plan, 78% ATM bid-ask, 41% NLV max loss, 4 DTE to earnings, implied 3.27% on name 2.09% from resistance. AZZ: reward/risk 0.37 BELOW desk's own 0.5 minimum (should not have been queued), 150% ATM bid-ask, 46% NLV max loss. Both flagged reject with desk data + literature support.

Five sharpened filters proposed: no long-vol above 20% implied move; no long-vol when ATM spread >30%; no long-vol above 25% NLV max loss; no long-vol within 7d earnings unless 10-20% implied; reject anything below 0.5 R/R.

Honest framing on the 10%/month ambition: it puts you in 0.1-0.3% tail of retail; only path is process discipline that exceeds 99% of retail. At ,599: 10%/mo = ,020 (3.14x); 3%/mo = ,278 (1.42x); 1%/mo = ,803 (1.13x).

Noticed codex has multiple new modules in the working tree (inferno_dte_policy_analysis, inferno_expectancy_ledger, inferno_process_compliance, inferno_trade_evidence, inferno_trading_behavior_audit, plus modifications to inferno_paper_test_director, strike_selector, trade_management). Those are clearly the discipline-research operationalization in codex's lane (BACKLOG #9-#17). Not touching them; left for codex to commit. My doc is research-only, no code, no module wiring.

Research-only. promotable=False. authorityChanged=False. liveTradingAllowed=False. brokerSubmitAllowed=False. 30-outcome gate=1/30.

author: claude
title: Code-hygiene loose ends from threshold research (Codex-lane, surface-only)
body:
Found during the limits/thresholds walkthrough. All Codex/risk-lane; logged for action, NOT edited by Claude. Research-only; authority unchanged.

1. ORPHAN CONSTANT: inferno_math_config.MIN_WILSON_LOWER_FOR_EDGE = 0.42 is no longer consumed by any decision logic (only snapshot() and the score-threshold drift watch-list reference it). Post-Option-A it is misleading: a reader will think 0.42 is the live promotion win-rate floor, but the live gate in inferno_strategy_lab is now payoff-aware. It is also a same-value, different-name twin of strategy_lab.MIN_WIN_RATE_LOWER_BOUND (the 0.42 fallback), which the name-based drift guard cannot link. Fix: annotate/remove the orphan, or wire the gate to source it from math_config.

2. PAYOFF-RATIO OUTLIER SENSITIVITY in the shipped Option A gate: win_rate_floor_from_payoff() caps the target upper side (min 0.999) but has NO lower clamp, and payoff_ratio() uses mean-win / mean-loss. Long-premium winners are fat-tailed, so one outlier erodes the win-rate bar: clean 13W@2R/17L@-1R -> target 0.363; replace one 2R win with a 20R outlier -> payoff 3.38, target 0.258 (-10 pts on one tail); extreme payoff 30 -> target 0.062. Recommend a robust payoff estimator (median or winsorized win/loss) and/or an absolute lower clamp (~0.20) on winRateLowerBoundTarget. Keeps payoff-awareness, removes the outlier exploit. MIN_SCORED_TRADES=30 limits but does not prevent this.

3. TWO KELLY FORMULAS coexist: strategy_lab.kelly_from_returns uses discrete Kelly p-(1-p)/b; inferno_kelly_sizing uses Gaussian mean/variance. They can disagree on the same strategy. Decide which is authoritative for sizing.

Secondary (some already in docs/PROMOTION_GATE_WINRATE_FLOOR_2026-06-26.md): R-unit drawdown gate is inert vs the dollar account (-6R = 203 pct of NLV at $500/1R on $1477); 36h Schwab-chain freshness is loose for names inside ~2 days of earnings; spread tolerance mismatch (risk gate blocks >35 pct, but the quote-quality composite flags ~18 pct as poor/no-liquid). liveTradingAllowed=False; brokerSubmitAllowed=False; no risk constant changed.

title: Root cause of 1/30 paper stall = drawdown pause zeroing the cap for PAPER too (proposal: decouple)
body:
Traced why paper evidence is stuck at 1/30. The account is ~53% below peak (1686 -> 788), so the drawdown protocol is at level 'pause' (capMultiplier=0, newEntriesAllowed=false). effectiveCap = min(acked,config) * capMultiplier = $0, and inferno_risk_policy blocks EVERY candidate on 'max loss exceeds single-ticket cap $0.00'. Correct for LIVE capital. But the SAME gate governs PAPER (simulated) staging, so a real-money drawdown guard is blocking simulated evidence collection — which risks $0 and is exactly what's needed to escape 1/30.

Operator-authorized exploration. Wrote docs/PAPER_LIVE_DRAWDOWN_DECOUPLE_2026-07-03.md proposing a mode=paper|live on the risk gate: paper uses a fixed paper budget (BOOTSTRAP_TICKET_DOLLARS or up to ~500) and SKIPS the drawdown multiplier + evidence-adjusted-$0; live unchanged (fully paused at 53% dd). Keeps MAX_OPEN_PAPER_TICKETS, same-ticker guard, and all quote/liquidity gates in both modes. liveTradingAllowed/brokerSubmitAllowed stay false; no real dollar moves. Caveat: current names still fail liquidity gates, so this is the necessary structural half, not sufficient alone.

Also filed: ack floor bug — 'accept --floor X' records acceptedCap using the default $25 floor, so --floor mismatches the live recommendation and drops the cap. Secondary.

Housekeeping: I acked then tried to revoke the capital-scaling formula while diagnosing; sandbox couldn't unlink data/inferno_capital_scaling_ack.json (Operation not permitted). Operator should run 'python3 inferno_capital_scaling.py revoke' on the Mac to restore clean state — the ack is inert now (dd multiplier zeroes it). researchOnly; authorityChanged=False; liveTradingAllowed=False; brokerSubmitAllowed=False.

author: claude
title: Root cause of illiquid funnel: chain-pull ignores priority slate (fix spec: docs/UNIVERSE_LIQUIDITY_CHAIN_PULL_2026-07-03.md)
body:
Investigated why paper candidates are always thin/illiquid. It's a SELECTION bug, not market illiquidity. inferno_schwab_daily_ops.default_symbol_universe() builds the 12-name Schwab option-chain pull from execution queue + approval queue + manual watchlist only. It does NOT include the daily priority slate. So chains get pulled for held miners + watchlist (CCI,AZZ,AEHR,BMI,TXN,IREN,HIVE,TE,CLSK) while the top-priority candidates (AVGO #6, KEYS, OTEX, LUNR) get NO chain at all. Credit-spread candidates therefore only ever surface on illiquid held names and the risk gate correctly rejects them.

Evidence it's selection not reality: held miners IREN/TE have HEAVY option flow (top-contract OI 17k-100k, vol 9k+); the desk just never looks at its best-ranked liquid large-caps.

FIX (memo has code sketch): default_symbol_universe should blend held positions + top-N priority slate (from latest_snapshot rows, sorted by priority, drop setupRec==Avoid and daysUntilEarnings>21) + execution/approval + watchlist, dedup, clamp to limit 12. New helper top_priority_slate(). This is the missing piece — makes the chain pull cover tradeable large-caps.

Secondary (keep): small caps AEHR/BMI genuinely wide (correct to block); atmSpreadPct-as-%-of-premium penalizes cheap OTM legs (companion fix, lower leverage).

Why it matters: pairs with the paper/live decouple — decouple lets the simulator run during drawdown; this makes it run on TRADEABLE names. Evidence on liquid large-caps is worth something; evidence on 60%-spread miners is not. Both needed to move off 1/30. Research-only; ops-lane data-coverage change; no authority touched; liveTradingAllowed/brokerSubmitAllowed stay false.

author: claude
title: CANDIDATE EDGE FOUND: 7-14 DTE pre-earnings straddles on liquid large-caps (docs/CANDIDATE_EDGE_7_14_DTE_STRADDLE_2026-07-03.md)
body:
Mined the 150 closed outcomes (inferno_dte_policy_analysis.json). Exactly one cut shows a robust positive edge: Long Straddle entered 7-14 DTE before earnings. n=39, mean net-R +0.87, win 59%, bootstrap 95% lower bound +0.32, survives outlier removal (+0.49R excl top 3 wins). 7 distinct LIQUID large-caps (ACN/MRVL/DELL/ORCL/CIEN/HPE). Payoff 3.15:1, breakeven 24% vs 59% actual. Same strategy at 22-35 DTE is -0.41R/7% win — worst in the book; Vertical Debit -0.39R. The aggregate desk edge (~+0.09R, LB -0.14) is edgeless ONLY because it averages a strong narrow edge with clear losers. Mechanism: 7-14 DTE captures the pre-earnings IV expansion (vega) + move, avoiding 22-35 DTE theta bleed and 0-6 DTE IV crush.

CAVEATS (gate any excitement): (1) all 39 are risk-FAILED / non-admissible — full large-cap straddle debit exceeds the ticket cap, so it's an edge the ~$788 account can't afford; (2) shadow/simulated with MODELED friction — unproven on real fills; (3) 59% win is high for straddles — check the exit model isn't generous.

ACTION: make THIS the hypothesis the paper loop proves once decouple + priority-slate chain-pull ship — point paper staging at 7-14 DTE straddles on liquid large-caps, gather ~30 real-friction paper outcomes on this exact rule. Solve account-size via a cheaper proxy (7-14 DTE debit spread or lower-priced liquid name) that captures the same IV-ramp. Stop generating 22-35 DTE straddles and vertical debits (negative edge, diluting). Scrutinize the shadow exit model.

This connects everything: the decouple lets the simulator run; chain-pull puts liquid names in scope; this finding says exactly WHAT to test. Research-only; no real money; authority unchanged; liveTradingAllowed/brokerSubmitAllowed false.

title: Campaign plan written: prove-or-kill the 7-14 DTE straddle edge (docs/CAMPAIGN_PLAN_PROVE_OR_KILL_2026-07-03.md)
body:
Deep-research + planning session. Refined the candidate-edge finding with exit-model scrutiny: the shadow HOLDS straddles to expiration (exitDte -3, held ~14d through earnings), so the 59% win rate is the honest 'move covered the cost' number, NOT exit-timing optimism. Two real holes the shadow leaves: (1) friction NOT applied (frictionRealized=false — no bid/ask crossed), which is the biggest risk to the edge; (2) no dates on records, so regime-robustness across earnings cycles is unconfirmed.

Wrote a narrow campaign plan making one thing the whole goal: prove-or-kill the 7-14 DTE pre-earnings straddle on liquid large-caps. Phases: (0) ship decouple + chain-pull [in queue] -> (1) instrument paper loop at the hypothesis with REAL friction, two exit variants (hold-to-expiry vs day-after-earnings), plus an affordable proxy structure for the small account -> (2) accrue >=30 outcomes across >=2-3 earnings cycles -> (3) pre-registered decision gates. Gate C: net-R bootstrap LB>0 + PF>=1.25 consistent across cycles = tiny live pilot (separate explicit ack, min size); else KILL the strategy. Honest meta-gate named: if the best edge in months dies under real friction, that's the signal to stop, not chase the next hypothesis.

author: claude
title: Campaign refinement (operator insight): test structure family at 7-14 DTE — is the edge vega or direction?
body:
Operator asked whether there's a vertical-call edge or if it's straddle-bias. Data check: only 2 families in shadow — Long Straddle (100) and Vertical Debit (50). Vertical Debit loses where tested (22-35 DTE n=37 -0.40R) but has only n=2 in the 7-14 window — UNTESTED there, not disproven. Mechanistic hypothesis: the 7-14 DTE edge is a VEGA/IV-expansion effect. Net-vega ranking (straddle ~2x > strangle ~1.5x > single long ~1x > vertical ~0 net) MATCHES the outcomes (straddle wins, vertical loses) — the vertical is the one structure that discards the vega, which is why a vertical is the WRONG cheap proxy. The right affordable proxy that preserves the edge is a SINGLE LONG option or STRANGLE (cheaper than straddle, still long vega, fits account + paper budget).

Added a structure-family arm to the campaign plan: at 7-14 DTE on the same liquid large-caps with real friction, test straddle / strangle / single-long-call / single-long-put / vertical-debit. If long-vega structures win but vertical loses -> edge is vega, trade cheapest long-vega structure (single long option). If vertical also wins -> edge is timing/direction, cheap vertical is the workhorse. Either answer decisively solves the account-size problem. research-only; authority unchanged.

author: claude
title: MAJOR: desk over-counts evidence (trades not events) - inflates every CI, weakens 30-gate; the straddle edge evaporates (docs/EVIDENCE_OVERCOUNT_CLUSTERING_2026-07-06.md)
body:
Deep dig requested by operator on the win-rate/beat-rate tension. Found a systemic methodology flaw bigger than any single edge:

The desk counts TRADES as independent. They aren't. 150 closed outcomes = only 18 distinct tickers (8.3 trades/name; CIEN 13x, ORCL 12x, ACN 12x). Repeat trades on the same name around the same earnings are highly correlated -> every bootstrap CI is too tight, and the 30-outcome promotion gate counts trades (could be cleared by ~5 names x repeats).

PROOF (the straddle edge dissolved): 7-14 DTE straddle by-trade n=39 mean +0.87 LB +0.32 (looked robust) -> cluster-bootstrap BY NAME (7 names) median +0.80 CI [-0.18, +2.40], LB below zero. Two names carried it (DELL +4.45, HPE +2.35), other 5 near-zero/negative. Matched test (3 names in both 7-14 and 22-35 windows): 7-14 was WORSE all 3 -> the DTE 'gradient' was name-selection, not timing. So the edge = ~2 lucky names in a small correlated sample.

FIX (strengthens the gate, not a loosening): (1) add event id = ticker+earnings-date to records; (2) promotion gate counts DISTINCT EVENTS not trades (>=20-25 events); (3) cluster-robust bootstrap (resample events not trades) everywhere a CI gates a decision - add cluster_bootstrap() to math_config, route strategy_lab/evidence_strength/promotion_gap/score_calibration through it; (4) cap per-event weight in cohort means.

IMPACT: downgrade the 7-14 straddle from 'robust edge' to 'weak unproven lead, not distinguishable from no edge'. Campaign must accrue DISTINCT events (>=30 across cycles), not repeats on a few names. This is very likely why the desk keeps surfacing edges that aren't real. My own audit checks carry the same caveat. researchOnly; authority unchanged; makes the evidence bar harder+honest.

title: Ship-ready spec: auto-approve paper tickets capped by distinct event (docs/AUTO_APPROVE_PAPER_EVENT_CAP_2026-07-06.md)
body:
Operator asked to spec auto-approving paper tickets. Key reframe: auto-approval already exists (AUTO_PAPER_SELECTION_ENABLED=on; director auto-stages gate-passing candidates as auto-paper-selected at line ~290). The human is not the gate. The old 'approval-missing' was the $0 cap upstream, now fixed by the decouple.

The real fix is NOT more auto-approval but auto-approval WITH a distinct-event cap, so the loop doesn't mass-produce correlated pseudo-evidence (the over-count flaw: 150 outcomes = 18 names). Spec: define event_id = ticker + earnings-date; at the auto-select branch, count existing tickets on that event (open+scored); auto-stage only if under MAX_PAPER_TICKETS_PER_EVENT (new config, default 2); else category 'event-capped' (benign). Prioritise zero-ticket events in the staging sort so the budget widens coverage. Stamp eventId on every staged ticket - same field then powers the promotion gate's distinct-event count and cluster bootstrap (clustering memo). Keep all existing guards (same_ticker_open, MAX_OPEN_PAPER_TICKETS, quality/cap gates). 5 acceptance tests incl authority-invariant.

Together with the decouple (paper runs during drawdown) + priority-slate chain-pull (liquid names), this lets the campaign accrue DISTINCT-event evidence autonomously - the thing that's been impossible - and hitting '30' will mean 30 independent bets. Paper-only, simulated, no real money; liveTradingAllowed/brokerSubmitAllowed stay false.

author: claude
title: Current 0-tickets bottleneck = liquidity metric miscalibration (flags GOOG as thin); recalibrate to spread-primary (docs/LIQUIDITY_METRIC_MISCALIBRATION_2026-07-06.md)
body:
After decouple+chain-pull+$2000 budget shipped, campaign still stages 0 paper tickets. Root cause: the atmLiquidityScore/thin-atm-liquidity gate is miscalibrated and rejects the liquid large-caps the campaign needs. Live data: GOOG (ATM spread 4.7%, top OI 7279/vol 8275) scores 61 and is FLAGGED thin-atm-liquidity; meme-miner IREN (retail frenzy OI/vol 16k) scores 90 'usable'; TXN scores 20 because the single ATM strike was a low-OI hole. thin-atm-liquidity fires on 10 of 12 names incl GOOG+TXN.

Three flaws in liquidity_score_for_contract (spread band + raw OI + raw volume, threshold 70): (1) conflates speculative volume/OI flurry with execution quality -> ranks meme-miner above Google; (2) 70 threshold over-rejects -> if GOOG can't pass, nothing does; (3) ATM-strike-fragile -> one low-OI strike tanks a liquid name.

FIX (recalibrate quality gate, not a risk loosening): (1) make ATM bid/ask SPREAD the primary tradeability gate (<=~10-12%; GOOG 4.7% & FCX 6.5% pass, TXN 16.8% & wide miners fail), OI as secondary floor, don't let raw volume lift a wide-spread name; (2) lower/replace the 70 composite threshold; (3) compute ATM from a window of nearest 3-5 strikes (median), not one strike; (4) KEY: add a reference-basket test - GOOG/AAPL/MSFT/SPY MUST pass on normal chains, else the gate is miscalibrated by construction. Keep rejecting truly wide names (BMI 98%, AZZ 32%). Paper-only; no authority change; liveTradingAllowed/brokerSubmitAllowed false.
